_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: Abdominal lump in right upper quadrant. Pain may be characterized as heaviness in abdomen. Associated symptoms dyspepsia, anorexia, nausea and vomiting
Obstructive jaundice when daughter cyst may communicate with biliary tree
Anaphylactic shock if cyst ruptures into peritoneal cavity

Other less common complications include intraperitoneal rupture, intrathoracic rupture, internal rupture, rupture into viscera, rupture into vascular system and external compression leading to portal hypertension
Symptomatic and complicated cases are modality of choice for surgery

ppd_preauth.q: Was the indication for surgery mentioned?
Did the USG/CT/MRI Abdomen report confirm the diagnosis?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed ICPs with daily vitals and line of treatment?
Are the detailed procedure / Operative Notes / photographs and histopathology report available?
Is the Discharge summary with follow-up advice at the time of discharge?

common_queries: Indication for surgery not mentioned in clinical notes
USG/CT/MRI Abdomen report does not confirm diagnosis
Clinical notes are incomplete (missing detailed history, signs, symptoms, or indication for procedure)
Detailed ICPs with daily vitals and line of treatment are missing or incomplete
Detailed procedure/operative notes, photographs (if applicable), or histopathology report are missing
Discharge summary is missing or does not include follow-up advice

clinical_key_pointers: Hydatid disease in people is mainly caused by infection with the larval stage of the dog tapeworm Echinococcus granulosus.
It is an important pathogenic, zoonotic and parasitic infection (acquired from animals) of humans, following ingestion of tapeworm eggs excreted in the faeces of infected dogs.
Cystic echinococcosis most frequently involves only one organ by forming a solitary cyst.
Hydatid disease commonly involves the liver (75%) and lungs (15%), followed by other regions of the body (10- 15%).
surgical_options: PAIR- Puncture, Aspiration, Injection (of a scolicidal agent), Reaspiration
Anatomical or non-anatomical liver resections, including cystopericystectomy
